Meaning / Significance: The new moon appears. A day of darkness associated with Whiro, the embodiment of evil and misfortune. Rest.
Recommended Activities: Rest, spiritual reflection

Meaning / Significance: The first sliver of light returns. Energy begins to build slowly.
Recommended Activities: Light preparation work, planning

Meaning / Significance: Light increases and warms. The name suggests gleaming or shining.
Recommended Activities: Fishing, beginning new work, light planting

Meaning / Significance: Favourable energy continues to build.
Recommended Activities: Ocean fishing, planting near-surface crops, gathering

Meaning / Significance: Energy is strong and positive. An excellent day.
Recommended Activities: Planting root crops, deep-sea fishing, all productive work

Meaning / Significance: First of the Tamatea nights — associated with the atua Tamatea. Unsettled weather and energy.
Recommended Activities: Rest, indoor work
Avoid: The sea, important tasks, new ventures

Meaning / Significance: A calmer Tamatea night — aio means peaceful. The disruption eases.
Recommended Activities: Light tasks, cautious fishing

Meaning / Significance: The last and most intense Tamatea night — whakapau means to exhaust completely.
Recommended Activities: Rest, spiritual reflection

Meaning / Significance: Things hidden are revealed. A transitional night.
Recommended Activities: Divination, seeking lost things, moderate tasks

Meaning / Significance: The chiefly night — ariki means chief. Very favourable. High energy and good fortune.
Recommended Activities: All activities — planting, fishing, travel, ceremonies

Meaning / Significance: Abundance and fruitfulness. Excellent conditions.
Recommended Activities: Planting, harvesting, ocean fishing, communal gatherings

Meaning / Significance: The moon is fattening, growing full. Good general conditions.
Recommended Activities: All productive work, fishing, planting above-ground crops

Meaning / Significance: The moon is nearly full. Good energy but beginning to peak.
Recommended Activities: Fishing, gathering, preparation work

Meaning / Significance: Standing firm — turu means a prop or support. Stable, reliable.
Recommended Activities: Planting, building, steady productive work

Meaning / Significance: The full moon — rākau nui, the great tree. Most favourable night of the entire cycle.
Recommended Activities: All activities highly favoured — plant, fish, gather, celebrate

Meaning / Significance: The night after full moon. Very favourable — energy of Rākaunui lingers.
Recommended Activities: Continue all good work from Rākaunui

Meaning / Significance: Good for fishing and ocean activities. Waning moon pulls water and draws fish.
Recommended Activities: Fishing (especially net fishing and diving), ocean activities

Meaning / Significance: Energy begins to diminish. Still workable but waning.
Recommended Activities: Moderate tasks, harvesting, indoor work

Meaning / Significance: The void begins — korekore means nothingness. Te whiwhia: nothing is obtained.
Recommended Activities: Rest, reflection

Meaning / Significance: The void continues. Te rawea: nothing to delight in.
Recommended Activities: Spiritual work, rest, storytelling, wānanga

Meaning / Significance: Drawing near to Tangaroa (god of the sea). Ocean energy returns.
Recommended Activities: Preparation for fishing, mending nets, ocean observation

Meaning / Significance: First night of Tangaroa — god of the sea ascendant.
Recommended Activities: Ocean fishing, river fishing, shellfish gathering

Meaning / Significance: Tangaroa within — deep ocean energy. Very good for fishing.
Recommended Activities: Deep-sea fishing, diving, all ocean activities

Meaning / Significance: Last night of Tangaroa. Kiokio (a fern) unfurls — final opening of this energy.
Recommended Activities: Fishing, kaimoana gathering, water-related activities

Meaning / Significance: Night of Tāne — god of forests, birds, and light.
Recommended Activities: Planting, forest gathering, bird work, land-based activities

Meaning / Significance: Great listening — ōrongo means to listen, nui means great.
Recommended Activities: Planting, decisions, community meetings, all productive work

Meaning / Significance: Life force — the mauri (essence) of the moon fades. Transitional.
Recommended Activities: Complete ongoing tasks, prepare for the new cycle

Meaning / Significance: The land is cut off — mutu (ended) whenua (land). The darkest night.
Recommended Activities: Rest, spiritual reflection

Meaning / Significance: The looking — the moon watches for its own return. A liminal night between cycles.
Recommended Activities: Rest, contemplation, await the new moon
